title: QFT Interacting Deep: Wilson RG, Fixed Points & Universality Classes

key_theorem.statement: Fixed points of the Wilson RG are EML-classified by the algebraic accessibility of their critical exponents: Gaussian (EML-0), ε-expansion Wilson-Fisher (EML-2), exact 2D Ising (EML-3), 3D Ising/bootstrap (EML-∞). The approach to any fixed point is EML-1 (exponential in RG time t = ln μ). The KT transition has EML-2 essential singularity: ln ξ = c(T-T_KT)^{-1/2}.

gaussian_fp.name: Gaussian (free) fixed point
gaussian_fp.stability: unstable in d<4, marginal in d=4

wilson_fisher_fp.name: Wilson-Fisher fixed point
wilson_fisher_fp.eml_reason: g* = ε/6: rational in ε = EML-2; η = ε²/54 = polynomial = EML-2

wilson_rg.eml_rg_time: t = ln(s): EML-2 logarithm is the RG clock
wilson_rg.eml_approach: g(t) → g* exponentially → EML-1 decay toward fixed point

universality_classes.name: Mean-field / Landau
universality_classes.eml_reason: Free Gaussian theory: no interactions, EML-0 fixed point

universality_classes.name: 2D Ising (exact)
universality_classes.eml_reason: Onsager solution via spinors: critical correlators = EML-3 (conformal blocks with sin/cos)

universality_classes.name: 3D Ising (CFT bootstrap)
universality_classes.eml_reason: No closed form; bootstrap gives ν≈0.6299 numerically → EML-∞ (no EML-finite formula known)

universality_classes.name: 2D XY / Kosterlitz-Thouless
universality_classes.eml_reason: KT transition: correlation length ξ ~ exp(c/√(T-T_KT)) = EML-1 singular behavior; η=1/4 at T_KT

universality_classes.name: O(N) large-N
universality_classes.eml_reason: Large-N solvable: ν = 1/(d-2) in 2<d<4 → rational → EML-2

universality_classes.name: Wilson-Fisher (d=4-ε)
universality_classes.eml_reason: ε-expansion: g*=ε/6, ν=1/2+ε/12, η=ε²/54 — polynomials in ε = EML-2

kosterlitz_thouless.eml_reason: ln ξ = c/√(T-T_KT): EML-2 (inverse square root of distance from T_KT). The essential singularity is an EML-2 divergence in ln ξ, not ξ itself.
kosterlitz_thouless.kt_vs_normal_transition: Normal transition: ξ ~ (T-T_c)^{-ν} = EML-2 power law. KT transition: ln ξ ~ (T-T_KT)^{-1/2} = EML-2 — same depth but different form.

ope_2d_ising.reason: Onsager: Δ_σ=1/8, exact via spinors → EML-3

ope_3d_ising.reason: Δ_σ known only from bootstrap numerics → EML-∞

operator_product_expansion.eml_principle: OPE coefficients are EML-2 when scaling dimensions are rational/algebraic; EML-∞ when dimensions are only numerically accessible (e.g. 3D Ising bootstrap)

eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Exponential approach to fixed point g(t)→g*; vortex fugacity exp(-E_c/T)
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Wilson-Fisher exponents (ε-expansion); KT ln ξ singularity; O(N) large-N
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: 2D Ising exact (Onsager spinors); OPE in exactly solvable CFTs
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: 3D Ising bootstrap exponents; OPE of numerically-only CFTs

connections.to_session_75: Session 75: running coupling = EML-2. Session 84: Wilson-Fisher fixed point = EML-2 (same depth class)
connections.to_session_57: Session 57: Boltzmann = EML-1. Session 84: approach to fixed point = EML-1 same universality
